items.question: How many executive departments does the White House describe as part of the executive branch Cabinet structure?
items.explanation: The president appoints the heads of 15 executive departments.

items.question: The heads of the main executive departments together are commonly called what?
items.explanation: The Cabinet is made up of the leaders of the major executive departments.

items.question: What do federal agencies mainly do?
items.correctAnswer: carry out and administer laws and programs
items.explanation: Agencies help implement laws and manage the daily work of government.

items.question: What is an independent agency?
items.correctAnswer: a federal agency outside the Cabinet departments
incorrectAnswers: a private business hired by Congress
a state court with federal powers
items.explanation: Independent agencies are federal bodies that are not organized as Cabinet departments.

items.question: What is one reason governments use agencies and departments?
items.correctAnswer: to handle specialized work on a large scale
items.explanation: Modern governments rely on specialized offices to manage complex programs and services.

items.question: Which branch contains most federal agencies and departments?
items.explanation: Most agencies and departments sit within the executive branch and help enforce federal law.
